old_claim.note: Castle Garrison Logistics is contextual infrastructure or shared knowledge, not a strict hard prerequisite.

new_claim.note: Tax Accounting is the general administrative-record context for tracking tax income, leases, rights, and maintenance obligations around wall upkeep.

source_shape.source_locator: City of York Council says regular maintenance orders were issued and that 14th-century documents record grazing rights and leased income for the whole wall circuit.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source describes administrative orders, documents, rights, and leased income for maintaining the wall circuit.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Tax accounting better matches recorded income rights and maintenance orders; castle-garrison logistics concerns defended castles and garrison supply, not municipal wall-maintenance finance.

ontology_before: Town Wall Maintenance Taxes depended on Castle Garrison Logistics, conflating municipal wall-maintenance finance with operational support for defended castles and garrisons.
ontology_after: Town Wall Maintenance Taxes depends on Tax Accounting for the administrative record and income-tracking context documented by York's 14th-century wall records.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: town_wall_maintenance_taxes now depends on tax_accounting and no longer directly depends on castle_garrison_logistics.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to castle garrison supply arrangements rather than municipal wall taxes and records.
A stronger source showed York wall-maintenance finance depended on castle-garrison provisioning rather than tax and income accounting.
The tax_accounting node were narrowed away from tracking tax income, rights, obligations, and arrears.
short_reason: York wall records fit tax accounting, not castle garrison logistics.
